description:
Goal
Eliminate the semantic-analysis churn that causes Enum.with_index/2 with a mapper closure to time out in compiler2, then re-enable the temporarily disabled backend-interp regression test.

Problem being fixed
This is not a runtime-demand/backend/native bug. --log-telemetry shows compiler2 timing out in semantic analysis with repeated AnalyzeActivation retractions and redefinitions through the Enum.to_list -> Enum.reverse -> Enum.reduce -> Enumerable(List).reduce -> List.reduce_cont/reduce_step SCC. The ownership-boundary/runtime-transport arc should finish first where possible; this follow-on tracks the separate activation/type-growth bug so we do not conflate it with provider-boundary or transport modeling bugs.

Disabled test to re-enable
- src/compiler2/drive_test.rs: compiler2_interp_runs_enum_with_index_mapper_from_backend_artifacts

Observed failure
- Repro source:
  fn main() do
    dbg(Enum.with_index(["a", "b"], fn (x, _index) -> x <> "!" end))
  end
- fz2 command:
  target/debug/fz2 --log-telemetry /tmp/fz2-enum-with-index.jsonl interp /tmp/fz2-enum-with-index.fz
- Current result:
  compiler2 root 0 exceeded 5000 ms drive limit after 7845 jobs with 11 pending
- Telemetry shape:
  - dominated by activation_analysis.defined / return_type.defined / callsite.defined churn
  - SealSemanticClosure(RootId(0)) stalls waiting on ActivationAnalyzed(FunctionId(74), input [Ty(22)])
  - hot functions map to Enum.to_list/1, Enum.reverse/1, Enum.reduce/3, Enum.reduce_finish/1, the reducer lambdas in Enum, and List.reduce/3, List.reduce_cont/3, List.reduce_step/3

Required work
- Read the telemetry trace and identify the exact semantic fact or type-instantiation cycle that keeps retracting the hot activation SCC.
- Minimize the repro if possible without losing the churn.
- Fix the semantic/type model in the right place; do not add timeouts, caps, or local special-cases for Enum.with_index.
- Re-enable compiler2_interp_runs_enum_with_index_mapper_from_backend_artifacts and make it pass for the right reason.

Checks
- cargo test compiler2_interp_runs_enum_with_index_mapper_from_backend_artifacts --lib
- target/debug/fz2 --log-telemetry <path> interp <repro> completes without the drive timeout
- telemetry no longer shows unbounded AnalyzeActivation retraction/redefine churn for the hot Enum/List SCC
title: Compiler2 semantic analysis: Enum.with_index mapper activation churn times out
